entry.title: Entry #40 in Morand v. Tesla, Inc., 1:25-cv-01213
entry.summary: Order for Scheduling Recommendations/Proposed Scheduling Order. Scheduling recommendations/proposed scheduling order due to the Court within sixty (60) days after the appearance of any defendant. Scheduling Recommendations/Proposed Scheduling Order due by 7/17/2026. Signed by Judge Susan Hightower. (cnr)

entry.title: Entry #27 in Morand v. Tesla, Inc., 1:25-cv-01213
entry.summary: Unopposed MOTION for Hearing re 24 MOTION to Dismiss Plaintiffs' Amended Complaint by Ashok Elluswamy, Lars Moravy, Elon Musk, Vaibhav Taneja, Tesla, Inc.. (Attachments: # 1 Proposed Order)(Bash, John) (Entered: 04/22/2026)

entry.title: Minute entry from 2026-04-21 in Morand v. Tesla, Inc., 1:25-cv-01213
entry.summary: Deficiency Notice

entry.title: Entry #26 in Morand v. Tesla, Inc., 1:25-cv-01213
entry.summary: ORDER GRANTING 23 Motion to Appear Pro Hac Vice for Attorney Sasha Boutilier for Ashok Elluswamy,Sasha Boutilier for Lars Moravy,Sasha Boutilier for Elon Musk,Sasha Boutilier for Vaibhav Taneja,Sasha Boutilier for Tesla, Inc.. Pursuant to our Administrative Policies and Procedures for Electronic Filing, the attorney hereby granted to practice pro hac vice in this case must register for electronic filing with our court within 10 days of this order. Registration is managed by the PACER Service Center. Signed by Judge Robert Pitman. (cnr) (Entered: 04/22/2026)

entry.title: Minute entry from 2026-04-20 in Morand v. Tesla, Inc., 1:25-cv-01213
entry.summary: DEFICIENCY NOTICE to John Bash: re 24 MOTION to Dismiss Plaintiffs' Amended Complaint. This filing is deficient. The caption of this pleading is requesting oral argument. This request should be filed as a motion for hearing/oral argument, noting if it is opposed/unopposed with a proposed order attached. (cnr)

entry.title: Entry #25 in Morand v. Tesla, Inc., 1:25-cv-01213
entry.summary: Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice and Consideration of Documents Incorporated by Reference in Support of Motion to Dismiss Plaintiffs' Amended Complaint Dkt. 24 by Ashok Elluswamy, Lars Moravy, Elon Musk, Vaibhav Taneja, Tesla, Inc. (Bash, John) Modified on 4/21/2026 to add motion event (cnr). (Entered: 04/20/2026)

entry.title: Entry #24 in Morand v. Tesla, Inc., 1:25-cv-01213
entry.summary: MOTION to Dismiss Plaintiffs' Amended Complaint by Ashok Elluswamy, Lars Moravy, Elon Musk, Vaibhav Taneja, Tesla, Inc.. (Attachments: # 1 Appendix A - Chart of Alleged Misstatements, # 2 Declaration of John F. Bash In Support of Defendants' Motion to Dismiss Plaintiff's Amended Complaint, # 3 Exhibit 1 - Tesla 2023 Form 10-K (Jan. 29, 2024) (excerpted), # 4 Exhibit 2 - Tesla 2022 Form 10-K (Feb. 7, 2023) (excerpted), # 5 Exhibit 3 -Tesla 2024 Form 10-K (Jan. 31, 2025) (excerpted), # 6 Exhibit 4 - Tesla Q1 2024 Presentation (Apr. 23, 2024) (excerpted), # 7 Exhibit 5 - Tesla Q4 2024 Presentation (Jan. 29, 2025) (excerpted), # 8 Exhibit 6 - Tesla Q3 2024 Earnings Call Transcript (Oct. 23, 2024), # 9 Exhibit 7 - Tesla Q4 2024 Earnings Call Transcript (Jan. 29, 2025), # 10 Exhibit 8 - Piper Sandler analyst report (May 5, 2025), # 11 Exhibit 9 - Guggenheim Securities analyst report (June 11, 2025), # 12 Exhibit 10 - Tesla, Inc. Historical Price Data (Yahoo Finance), # 13 Exhibit 11 - Tesla Q1 2021 Form 10-Q (Apr. 17, 2021), # 14 Exhibit 12 - Tesla "Vision Update" Blog Post (Oct. 31, 2022), # 15 Exhibit 13 - Lidar News article dated Aug.19, 2022, # 16 Exhibit 14 - Electrek article dated Nov. 2, 2016, # 17 Exhibit 15 - Tesla Q1 2025 Earnings Call Transcript (Apr. 22, 2025), # 18 Exhibit 16 - Forbes article dated (Mar. 13, 2021), # 19 Exhibit 17 - Tesla Q2 2024 Earnings Call Transcript (Jul. 23, 2024), # 20 Exhibit 18 - SAE Levels of Driving Automation Chart, # 21 Exhibit 19 - Yahoo! Finance article dated Jan. 23, 2026, # 22 Exhibit 20 - CNBC article dated Feb. 7, 2022, # 23 Exhibit 21 - Wolfe Research Analyst Report dated Jan. 30, 2025, # 24 Exhibit 22 - Tesla Q1 2023 Earnings Call Transcript (Apr. 19, 2023), # 25 Exhibit 23 - Tesla Q1 2024 Earnings Call Transcript (Apr. 23, 2024), # 26 Exhibit 24 - CNBC interview transcript dated May 20, 2025, # 27 Exhibit 25 - CNBC interview transcript dated May 20, 2025, # 28 Exhibit 26 - 2025 CEO Performance Award Agreement (Ex. 4.4 to Teslas Nov. 7, 2025 Form S-8), # 29 Exhibit 27 - Fortune article dated May 26, 2025, # 30 Exhibit 28 - Federal Highway Administration Statistics, # 31 Exhibit 29 - Electrek article dated June 20, 2025, # 32 Exhibit 30 - CNBC article dated June 20, 2025, # 33 Exhibit 31 - Reuters article dated June 20, 2025, # 34 Exhibit 32 - Investopedia article dated June 24, 2025, # 35 Exhibit 33 - CNBC article dated June 24, 2025, # 36 Exhibit 34 - Fortune article dated June 24, 2025, # 37 Exhibit 35 - Yahoo! Finance article dated June 25, 2025, # 38 Exhibit 36 - CNBC article dated June 25, 2025, # 39 Proposed Order)(Bash, John) (Entered: 04/20/2026)

entry.summary: MOTION to Appear Pro Hac Vice by John Franklin Bash for Sasha Boutilier ( Filing fee $ 100 receipt number ATXWDC-21797908) by on behalf of Ashok Elluswamy, Lars Moravy, Elon Musk, Vaibhav Taneja, Tesla, Inc.. (Bash, John) (Entered: 04/20/2026)

entry.title: Minute entry from 2026-04-14 in Morand v. Tesla, Inc., 1:25-cv-01213
entry.summary: Order on Motion for Miscellaneous Relief

entry.title: Minute entry from 2026-04-14 in Morand v. Tesla, Inc., 1:25-cv-01213
entry.summary: Text Order GRANTING 22 Joint Motion to Extend Page Limits entered by Judge Robert Pitman. For good cause shown, IT IS ORDERED that Defendants' motion to dismiss the Amended Complaint, if any, may be no more than 30 pages; Lead Plaintiffs' response to Defendants' motion to dismiss the Amended Complaint may be no more than 30 pages; and Defendants' reply brief in support of their motion to dismiss the Amended Complaint may be no more than 15 pages. (This is a text-only entry generated by the court. There is no document associated with this entry.)(JMlc)

entry.title: Entry #22 in Morand v. Tesla, Inc., 1:25-cv-01213
entry.summary: STIPULATION and Proposed Order Regarding Page Limits for Briefing on Defendants' Motion to Dismiss by Ashok Elluswamy, Lars Moravy, Elon Musk, Vaibhav Taneja, Tesla, Inc.. (Bash, John) (Entered: 04/13/2026)

entry.title: Entry #21 in Morand v. Tesla, Inc., 1:25-cv-01213
entry.summary: ORDER GRANTING 17 Motion for Michael J. Wernke to Appear Pro Hac Vice for Amiri Gandhi and Jacob Soto. Pursuant to our Administrative Policies and Procedures for Electronic Filing, the attorney hereby granted to practice pro hac vice in this case must register for electronic filing with our court within 10 days of this order. Registration is managed by the PACER Service Center. Signed by Judge Robert Pitman. (tgp) (Entered: 02/24/2026)
